assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'optimal configuration combines Extended release channel with maintenance exclusions' and provides specific commands showing this combination

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and '--add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support' with explanation of auto-tracking End of Support

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response includes specific maintenance window configuration with '--maintenance-window-start "2026-01-01T02:00:00Z"' and '--maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA"' for Saturday windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes '--maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=7776000s' (90 days) and explains it provides '90-day minimum gap between control plane patches (configurable 0-90 days)'

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended channel specifically, mentions '24-month support window' and explains 'No cost during standard support period — additional charges only apply during extended support (months 15-24)'

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response explains that automatic security patches continue on control plane while blocking disruptive upgrades, and emphasizes maintaining security posture throughout

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response includes a 'Multi-Environment Strategy' section showing progressive rollout across dev/staging/prod with different patch intervals, and discusses cluster disruption budget via patch version disruption interval

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple complete gcloud commands with specific flags for maintenance exclusions, windows, and disruption intervals throughout the response
